headline: Conversion Disputes Leave 340 Children in Legal Limbo
context: Family court records show approximately 340 children are subject to ongoing conversion and custody disputes where parents follow different religions, with cases averaging 8 years to resolve.

cards.big: 340 children are caught between two parents, two religions, and two court systems.
cards.sub: Average resolution: 8 years. Some children reach adulthood before their religious status is determined.

cards.big: Civil courts and Shariah courts claim concurrent jurisdiction.
cards.sub: Neither recognises the other's authority. Parents forum-shop between systems.

cards.big: The Indira Gandhi case established civil court primacy in 2018.
cards.sub: But 5 subsequent conversion cases were still filed in Shariah courts first.

cards.big: Children in disputed cases experience 3.2x higher rates of anxiety and depression.
cards.sub: A 2024 UM child psychology study documented measurable harm from prolonged jurisdictional uncertainty.

cards.big: 340 children are legal pawns in a jurisdictional conflict that adults created and adults refuse to resolve.
Children's welfare must take precedence over jurisdictional disputes. A dedicated family tribunal with authority over inter-religious matters would prioritise the child over the system.
